denial_letter_text:
AETNA
PO Box 14079
Lexington, KY 40512

Date: June 4, 2026
Member Name: John Doe
Member ID: W123456789
Claim/Authorization Number: AUTH-987654321
Provider: Precision Orthopedics and Sports Medicine
Service Requested: Platelet-Rich Plasma (PRP) Injection, Achilles Tendon (CPT 0232T)
Date(s) of Service: TBD

NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Dear John Doe,

We received a prior authorization request for a Platelet-Rich Plasma (PRP) injection. We have reviewed the clinical information provided by your physician.

Your request is denied. This service is not medically necessary under your plan.

Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) considers PRP experimental and investigational for Achilles tendinopathy. The submitted records do not meet CPB criteria for coverage.

Furthermore, a less costly modality is appropriate. Plan policy requires a step-down therapeutic approach. A localized corticosteroid injection is required prior to authorization. The documentation provided does not show that you have tried and failed a localized corticosteroid injection.

Therefore, we cannot approve the requested PRP injection.

Reviewing Medical Director:
Dr. Smith

APPEAL RIGHTS
If you disagree with this decision, you have the right to appeal. You or your authorized representative must submit your internal appeal in writing within 180 days of receiving this notice. Please include any additional medical records or documentation that you believe supports your request.

Please send your written appeal to:
Aetna Appeals Department
PO Box 14463
Lexington, KY 40512

If you have any questions regarding this notice, please contact Member Services at the number on the back of your ID card.
clinical_context: The patient is a 48-year-old male presenting with chronic Achilles tendinopathy (M76.60), confirmed by ultrasound demonstrating severe tendinosis with marked neovascularity on May 12, 2026. He has failed a 16-week comprehensive regimen of eccentric loading physical therapy, NSAIDs, and activity modification with no improvement in his VISA-A score, which remains at 32/100. The provider has recommended a Platelet-Rich Plasma (PRP) injection as the next appropriate intervention. While the insurer's denial mandates a localized corticosteroid injection as a prerequisite step-therapy, this is directly contradicted by current orthopedic safety standards. Corticosteroid injections into the load-bearing Achilles tendon carry an unacceptably high risk of iatrogenic tendon rupture, which would require surgical repair and prolonged disability. Given the structural degradation already evident on the patient's ultrasound, a steroid injection is strictly contraindicated and medically unsafe. Therefore, PRP is the most clinically appropriate, medically necessary, and safe conservative intervention remaining for this patient's chronic condition.
perturbation_notes: Flaws are already integrated seamlessly into the base draft (ERISA disclosure rights omitted; Reviewing Medical Director listed solely as 'Dr. Smith' without specialty or license details).
